# Heavy doc

This document exceeds the repo-wide default budget and carries no override, so the plain over-budget branch fires on it.

Documentation written for a reading model competes with the task itself for a fixed context window. Every paragraph loaded into that window is a paragraph of working room the task no longer has, which is why the default ceiling exists and why exceeding it requires either a split or an argued override rather than a shrug.

A long document also truncates badly. When a retrieval step takes only the head of a file, everything the author saved for the closing section simply never arrives, and the reader proceeds on the half that did. Keeping documents inside a known ceiling is what keeps that failure mode rare, and keeping the ceiling honest is what keeps the documents inside it.
